# Product Requirements Document
# Generated by req-engine from raw input
# Feature: Equipment Home Page + Asset Module Redesign
# Date: 2026-04-25
# ---

## 1. Project Overview

SPM ONE is redesigning the Asset module's Equipment entry point from a direct dashboard editor
into an Equipment Home Page — an equipment-centric landing experience analogous to the global
Home module. The Equipment Home Page organizes dashboards (read-only popup viewer), equipment
metadata, and scoped tool previews in a single modern view. Actual dashboard editing is
delegated to a separate Workspace module (spec TBD). The change also adds equipment-level
filtering to the Data & Sync tool in the Tools module.

## 2. Goals & Success Criteria

1. Users navigating to an equipment from the asset hierarchy land on an Equipment Home Page,
   not directly in the dashboard editor.
2. Users can browse and open any equipment dashboard as a read-only popup without entering
   edit mode, and can apply Viewed Data (scenario overlays) within that popup.
3. Users can see all equipment dashboards organized into Recent and Favourite groups in a grid
   layout that mirrors the Home module's pattern.
4. Users can see equipment information inline with ghost/skeleton loading UI.
5. Users can see 4 equipment-scoped tool tiles (Data & Sync, Shift Log, Documents,
   What-If Scenario) and launch the relevant tool page with that equipment pre-selected.
6. The Data & Sync tool gains an equipment-level filter control; navigating from Equipment
   Home pre-applies that filter.
7. An "Edit Dashboards" action prominently navigates the user to the Workspace module.

## 3. Target Users

- **Primary user:** Integrity Engineer — browses equipment health, reviews dashboards read-only,
  and launches scoped tools for a specific equipment.
- **Secondary user:** Process Engineer — references What-If scenarios and documents scoped to a
  specific equipment from the Equipment Home Page.

## 4. Functional Requirements

FR-01: The system shall display an Equipment Home Page when a user navigates to an equipment
       asset from the Site → Plant → Equipment hierarchy in the Asset module.
FR-02: The Equipment Home Page shall contain four distinct sections: Dashboard Section,
       Equipment Information Section, Tools Section, and an Edit Dashboards action.
FR-03: The Dashboard Section shall display all dashboards belonging to the current equipment
       in a responsive card grid, grouped into "Recent" and "Favourites" subsections.
FR-04: The Dashboard Section grid layout shall reuse the DashboardCard component and visual
       pattern from the global Home module (thumbnails, labels, same card dimensions).
FR-05: The system shall allow users to click a dashboard card to open a read-only Dashboard
       Popup window overlaid on the Equipment Home Page.
FR-06: The Dashboard Popup shall render the full widget grid for the selected dashboard in a
       non-editable (view-only) state; all drag, resize, and add/remove widget controls shall
       be hidden.
FR-07: The Dashboard Popup shall include a Viewed Data control that allows the user to select
       which What-If scenario result data appears on the dashboard widgets, reusing the
       existing Viewed Data mechanism.
FR-08: The Dashboard Popup shall include generate-report and share actions consistent with
       the existing equipment dashboard flow.
FR-09: The Dashboard Popup shall include a visible close button that dismisses the popup and
       returns focus to the Equipment Home Page.
FR-10: The system shall allow only one Dashboard Popup open at a time.
FR-11: The Equipment Information Section shall display equipment metadata (name, type, site,
       plant, status indicators) using ghost/skeleton loading placeholders that match the
       mockup already present in the equipment dashboard's right-panel info area.
FR-12: The Tools Section shall display exactly four tool tiles in a modern grid layout:
       Data & Sync, Shift Log, Documents, What-If Scenario.
FR-13: The Data & Sync tile shall display a summary of the equipment's sync status
       (loaded files count, error count, last updated timestamp).
FR-14: The Documents tile shall display the count of documents tagged to the current
       equipment.
FR-15: The What-If Scenario tile shall display the count of scenarios for the current
       equipment and a brief summary of the most recent run result (status + key metric).
FR-16: The Shift Log tile shall render as a disabled/placeholder state with a "Coming Soon"
       label; it shall have no interactive navigation or action.
FR-17: Clicking the Data & Sync tile shall navigate the user to the Data & Sync page in the
       Tools module with the current equipment pre-selected in the equipment filter.
FR-18: Clicking the Documents tile shall navigate the user to the Documents page in the Tools
       module with the current equipment name pre-applied as the asset filter.
FR-19: Clicking the What-If Scenario tile shall navigate the user to the What-If Scenario
       tool page with the current equipment pre-selected.
FR-20: After the user navigates back from a tool page to the Equipment Home Page, the
       equipment context shall be preserved (no re-selection required).
FR-21: The Edit Dashboards action shall be displayed prominently (e.g., a primary button or
       clearly visible top-level action) and shall navigate the user to the Workspace module.
FR-22: The Workspace module navigation target shall be a stub/placeholder that renders a
       holding screen until the full Workspace module specification is delivered.
FR-23: The Data & Sync tool page (data-sync.tsx) shall add an equipment filter dropdown/select
       control that filters both the Data Status table and the Sync & FEA Jobs table to the
       selected equipment.
FR-24: The Data & Sync tool shall, when navigated to from the Equipment Home Page tool tile,
       automatically apply the filter to the originating equipment.

## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

NFR-01: Performance — Equipment Home Page shall reach interactive skeleton state within
        300 ms and swap to mock content within 1 500 ms on the static mock data layer.
NFR-02: Accessibility — All interactive elements (tool tiles, dashboard cards, popup close,
        Edit Dashboards button) shall have keyboard-accessible focus states and aria-labels.
NFR-03: Visual Consistency — Equipment Home Page shall reuse existing shadcn/ui primitives
        (Card, Badge, Skeleton, Dialog/Sheet for popup), Tailwind utilities, and the DashboardCard
        component; no new CSS systems shall be introduced.
NFR-04: Layout — Tools Section shall use a grid arrangement that makes all four tool tiles
        visible without scrolling on a 1 440 × 900 viewport.

## 6. Out of Scope

- Full Workspace module implementation (spec TBD; only a navigation stub is required now).
- Shift Log feature implementation (placeholder tile only; no data, no navigation).
- Backend/API persistence (mock data layer continues throughout).
- Equipment Home Page layout below 1 024 px wide viewports.
- Dashboard Popup editing capabilities (it is strictly read-only).
- Multi-popup support (only one popup may be open at a time).

## 7. Constraints

- Tech stack is fixed: Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui, Zustand.
- Navigation is store-driven (`currentView` + Zustand), not URL-driven; all cross-module
  navigation must go through existing store actions (`setCurrentView`, `setCurrentPath`, etc.).
- The existing Equipment Dashboard editor (`equipment-dashboard.tsx`) remains the edit
  destination until the Workspace module spec is delivered.
- Shift Log tile must render as a non-interactive placeholder only.
- All new components must satisfy `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` with no type errors.

## 8. Assumptions Made

- [ ] "Workspace module" maps to the `workspace` key already present in `AppModule.key` enum
      — confirm this is the correct target.
- [ ] The Equipment Home Page fully replaces the current direct-to-dashboard-editor entry
      point for the Assets navigation flow (not a parallel/optional path) — confirm.
- [ ] "Recent accessed dashboards" and "favourite dashboards" on Equipment Home Page reuse the
      existing `recentDashboards` / `favoriteDashboards` Zustand state — confirm; they may need
      separate per-equipment slices.
- [ ] "Equipment information" section content mirrors the right-panel equipment info mockup
      already in `equipment-dashboard.tsx` (ghost loading cards) — confirm exact fields.
- [ ] The Viewed Data, generate-report, and share actions in the Dashboard Popup reuse the
      existing mechanism from the Equipment Dashboard with no new backend calls — confirm.
- [ ] Tool navigation uses Zustand state to pass `equipmentId` as a pre-filter parameter
      (no URL query params since navigation is store-driven) — confirm mechanism.

## 9. Open Questions

- [ ] Does clicking "Edit Dashboards" navigate to the existing `equipment-dashboard.tsx`
      (temporarily repurposed as Workspace), or to a brand-new `/workspace` route that is
      scaffolded now and filled later? This determines whether `equipment-dashboard.tsx` needs
      a `viewMode: 'edit' | 'workspace'` gate.
- [ ] For "Recent accessed dashboards" — is recency per-user, per-session, or global?
      Currently all recents are in-memory Zustand and reset on page refresh.
- [ ] What preview fields should appear on the What-If Scenario tile? (scenario count only,
      or also last run status + top result metric?)
- [ ] Should the Equipment Home Page be the new landing when navigating via the sidebar
      asset hierarchy, OR only when clicking an equipment card in the Plant Overview?
      (Both entry points may need to be updated.)

## 10. Suggested Task Breakdown Hints

- Domain + docs: update ontology (Dashboard.isFavorite, Dashboard.lastAccessedAt, new
  business rules, AppModule workspace stub, Tool entity scoping) and PROJECT.md.
- Asset module routing: gate the equipment entry point to render EquipmentHomePage instead
  of EquipmentDashboard when navigating from hierarchy.
- EquipmentHomePage scaffold: shell layout with four sections (Dashboard, Info, Tools, Edit CTA).
- Dashboard Section: grid with Recent / Favourite grouping using DashboardCard component.
- Dashboard Popup: read-only dashboard renderer using Dialog/Sheet overlay + Viewed Data.
- Equipment Information Section: ghost/skeleton loading cards with metadata fields.
- Tools Section: 4-tile grid layout with preview data + navigation wiring.
- Data & Sync filter: add equipment dropdown to data-sync.tsx; wire pre-filter from store.
- Workspace navigation stub: Edit Dashboards button + placeholder Workspace view.
- Cross-navigation: Zustand equipment pre-filter mechanism shared across tool navigations.
